content:
written in the honest calligraphy of labor. I do not look at his face because I do not want to see the pleading or the confusion that a mouth can manufacture. I watch the fingers. They hover over the table like a mist, casting no shadow that makes sense with the morning sun.

I pick up my pen. The click of the mechanism is a sharp jolt against my thumb. I write, the nib digging deep into the paper
